name: Kabardian
notes: Grapheme inventory NOT YET SPECIFIED (stub). Kabardian (East Circassian) is written in a Cyrillic alphabet. It is a stub not because the script is exotic but because the orthography is overwhelmingly DIGRAPHIC and TRIGRAPHIC: a ~48-consonant inventory (ejectives, labialised and pharyngealised series) is mapped onto the Cyrillic base with multi-character sequences — кӏ, кӏу, къ, къу, кхъ, кхъу, гъ, гъу, лъ, лӏ, пӏ, тӏ, фӏ, хь, хъ, ху, цӏ, щӏ, ӏу — including the palochka ⟨Ӏ⟩, so a correct longest-match grapheme table has to be built and verified before any map can be claimed. Latin and Arabic-script orthographies exist historically (diaspora) and are not modelled here.

sources.author: Wikipedia contributors
sources.title: Kabardian language
sources.notes: Consonant and vowel inventory, and the Cyrillic alphabet with its digraphs and the palochka.
